description_long: This is a small, round brick kiosk with a conical, shingled roof that is heavily weathered and covered in moss. The structure sits on a square stone-paved base, with the brickwork showing signs of age and some discoloration near the bottom. There is a single green-painted wooden door on one side, and a rectangular window with a green frame on the opposite side. The roof is constructed from overlapping, curved tiles, many of which are stained with moss and lichen, giving the kiosk a rustic, aged appearance. The overall design is compact and functional, with no visible signage or decorative elements. The kiosk appears to be a standalone structure, likely used for small-scale commercial or utility purposes in an urban or park setting.
description: A small, round brick kiosk with a mossy conical shingled roof, green door, and a single window, set on a stone-paved base. The structure is weathered and rustic, with no visible signage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the round brick wall, a rectangular window with a green frame, and the mossy conical roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the continuous brickwork and the extensive moss growth on the roof, with no openings visible.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring the window more centrally, with the brickwork and mossy roof clearly visible.
description_view_3: View showing the green wooden door as the main entry, with the brick wall and mossy roof above.
attribute_roof_design: Conical, sloped roof made of overlapping curved tiles, heavily moss-covered, no overhangs or decorative elements
attribute_architectural_style: Traditional rustic, utilitarian with vernacular influences
